description: Story-driven, uses anecdotes and narrative arcs. Best for marketing, communications, creative industries.

examples.title: Narrative Cover Letter for Journalism Internship
examples.body:
30 April 2026

Dear Editor,

It started with a story about a borehole.

In my home village in Western Province, a broken borehole meant women walked six kilometres each morning to fetch water. When I was fifteen, I wrote about it for a school essay competition. I did not win, but a local NGO read my essay and repaired the borehole within the month. That was the moment I understood the power of a well-told story.

Today, I am a final-year journalism student at the University of Zambia, and I have spent four years honing the craft of storytelling. I have written for the campus newspaper, produced a radio documentary on youth unemployment, and interned at a community radio station where I covered local council meetings and breaking news.

What I offer your newsroom is not just reporting skills, it is a deep commitment to stories that matter. I believe journalism should illuminate, not just inform. I am particularly interested in investigative reporting on social justice issues, and I have the patience, persistence, and ethical grounding to pursue difficult stories responsibly.

I would welcome the opportunity to learn from your experienced journalists and to contribute fresh perspectives from a generation that is hungry for meaningful media.

Yours sincerely,

Lillian Hambayi

examples.title: Creative Application for Content Strategy Role
examples.body:
17 July 2026

Dear Creative Director,

I still remember the first brand that made me feel something.

It was a local soap company's advertisement during the rainy season. Instead of showing perfect models, they showed a mother drying her children's school uniforms by the charcoal stove, with the tagline: 'For the moments that matter.' I was twelve years old, and I cried. That advertisement taught me that great content is not about selling a product, it is about connecting with people's real lives.

That lesson has guided my approach to content creation ever since. At Mulungushi University, I studied Mass Communication and built a portfolio that includes video scripts, social media campaigns, and brand storytelling projects. For a student entrepreneurship competition, I created a content series for a fictional sustainable fashion brand that won first place for 'Best Digital Strategy.'

I am proficient in content planning, copywriting, video editing, and social media analytics. But more importantly, I understand that content strategy is about empathy, about putting yourself in the audience's shoes and asking: what do they need to hear?

Your agency's work on community-focused campaigns is exactly the kind of meaningful content I want to help create. I am eager to bring my creativity, my understanding of Zambian audiences, and my passion for storytelling to your team.

Warm regards,

Grace Kabungo

examples.title: Narrative Letter for Museum Internship
examples.body:
5 May 2026

Dear Ms. Chanda,

My grandmother had a clay pot that told a story.

It was not just a cooking vessel, it was a record of her mother's hands, her village's traditions, and a craft that stretched back generations. When I was a child, she would tell me stories about the symbols carved into the pot while she prepared nshima. Those moments planted a seed that grew into my passion for cultural preservation.

I am now a third-year History and Cultural Studies student at the University of Zambia, and I am applying for the internship position at the National Museum. I want to help preserve and share the stories that make us who we are.

At university, I have studied museology, conservation techniques, and oral history documentation. I have also volunteered at a community heritage project where I recorded oral histories from elderly residents in my home district, documenting traditions that are at risk of being lost. I have basic skills in cataloguing, digital archiving, and exhibition planning.

I am not just looking for work experience, I am looking for a place where my personal mission aligns with professional practice. Your museum is that place.

Thank you for considering my application.

Yours sincerely,

Mapalo Chanda
